name: Dialogue Choice
description: A UI interaction where the player selects a response or decision during dialogue.
design_notes: Dialogue choices should communicate tone, consequence, and availability without overloading the player. The UI must handle locked choices, timed responses, history, controller focus, localization, and consequences that may appear later.
design_purpose: Let players express intent or role-play through conversation.
Expose narrative branches, skill checks, costs, or reputation gates.
Make dialogue state readable while preserving story pacing.
player_fantasy: The player feels their words and social choices can shape the scene.

parameters.name: timed_choice_seconds
parameters.description: Optional deadline before a default or silence choice is selected.

parameters.name: choice_preview_detail
parameters.description: How much meaning the UI reveals before selection.

edge_cases: Timed choices during pause, subtitle review, or accessibility menus need fair timer behavior.
Locked choices should not leak spoilers unless the design intentionally shows requirements.
Voiceover, subtitle text, and abbreviated choice labels can imply different meanings if not reviewed together.
Networked or co-op dialogue needs rules for who chooses and whether others can vote.
common_bugs: Controller focus defaults to a destructive or costly choice.
Choice requirements are checked for display but not again when selected.
Localized text overflows and hides cost or reputation requirements.
Dialogue state advances before save data records the selected branch.
balancing_notes: Showing exact consequences supports planning; hiding them supports role-play and uncertainty.
Skill or reputation choices should feel like payoff for prior investment, not mandatory optimal routes.
accessibility_notes: Support subtitle sizing, dialogue history, timer disable options, and clear focus indicators.
Avoid presenting tone icons without text labels for players who cannot infer icon meaning.

implementation_notes.unity: Render choices from dialogue_node_id and choice_id records, then re-check conditions, costs, and reputation gates when the player commits. Save the selected branch before advancing the dialogue node.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep dialogue state in a narrative service with current_node_id, available choice IDs, and condition results. Control nodes should request a choice transaction rather than mutating story flags directly.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use dialogue data assets or tables keyed by node and choice IDs, evaluate Gameplay Tags or quest conditions for display, then validate again in the dialogue subsystem before applying consequences.
implementation_notes.web: Drive the UI from a dialogue state object and commit selections through a pure chooseDialogueOption(node_id, choice_id) transaction that records branch, flags, and history together.

relationships.reason: Dialogue choices need dialogue node ids, branch conditions, and committed choice history.
Committed choices can update faction reputation or delayed consequence state.
Choice lists need text scaling, focus order, and layout reflow so all options remain readable.

example_games.title: Mass Effect
example_games.note: Uses dialogue choices to steer tone and narrative outcomes.

example_games.title: Disco Elysium
example_games.note: Uses dialogue choices with skill and character-state checks.
